blogsPage.article6Excerpt: Easy stops, short itineraries and preparation tips for a lighter trip.

createBlogPage.seoDescription: Create a new article on VNSEEA with title, content, topic, tags and thumbnail.
createBlogPage.heroDescription: Draft the title, content, topic, hashtags and cover image before publishing to the Blogs feed.
createBlogPage.contentDescription: These fields simulate the create blog UI from audit P-25 and do not call the real API yet.
createBlogPage.tagsHelp: Tags will appear here after input, separated by commas.
createBlogPage.submitHint: Mock state: the form only simulates UI and does not submit yet.
createBlogPage.statReadingDescription: Estimated reading time from current content.
createBlogPage.statTagsDescription: Hashtags help people find the article.
createBlogPage.emptyPreviewExcerpt: The opening excerpt will appear in the preview so you can review reading flow before publishing.
createBlogPage.checkTitleDescription: Use at least 12 characters so the blog card is not empty or too short.
createBlogPage.checkContentDescription: Use at least 80 characters so the reader layout has main content.
createBlogPage.checkTopicTagsDescription: Choose the right category and add at least one hashtag.
createBlogPage.checkThumbnailDescription: A cover image helps the article stand out in the blogs list.
createBlogPage.draftSaved: Mock draft saved. The content remains in the current page state.
createBlogPage.publishComplete: Mock publish complete. When the API is connected, this button will send data to blogs.php.
createBlogPage.publishMissing: Complete the missing checklist items before real publishing.
createBlogPage.demoContent:
When residents participate from the beginning, tree planting, waste sorting and public cleanliness plans are easier to maintain. This article records how a small group divides roles, keeps an update schedule and measures results after each week of work.

## Highlights
- Each group has one person responsible for reminders.
- Results are updated with before and after photos.
- Small activities are repeated consistently instead of being organized too large.

readBlogPage.notFound: This slug was not found in mock data, so the nearest sample article is open.
readBlogPage.author: Author
readBlogPage.authorDescription: This author contributes featured articles to the VNSEEA Blogs feed.
readBlogPage.comment1Body: The reader layout is easy to follow, and the related section on the right helps move to the next article quickly.
readBlogPage.comment2Body: The mock content is useful for testing the reader, reactions and comments before connecting the real API.
readBlogPage.article1Body1: Demand for recycled plastics is rising as more businesses move toward clearer sustainability goals. Instead of treating recycled material as a secondary choice, many supply chains are starting to include recycled plastic pellets in long-term purchasing plans.
readBlogPage.article1Body2: The biggest shift is input quality. Manufacturers need stable material sources, transparent sorting processes and better traceability to meet international partner standards.
readBlogPage.article1Body3: Over the next few years, the market may keep growing if businesses can balance cost, quality and the ability to prove environmental impact with reliable data.
readBlogPage.article2Body1: Industrial and automotive gas hoses are not just replacement accessories. They directly affect operational safety, material durability and pressure tolerance across different environments.
readBlogPage.article2Body2: When choosing a manufacturer, companies usually care about inspection, material standards, delivery time and support for custom production batches.
readBlogPage.article2Body3: A clear evaluation process reduces maintenance risk and keeps system performance stable.
readBlogPage.article3Body1: After work, young professionals often do not lack resources; they lack a sustainable learning rhythm. Short classes, small goals and study groups with fixed schedules make learning easier to maintain.
readBlogPage.article3Body2: A productive week does not necessarily require many study hours. What matters is choosing the right skill, recording progress and producing something small after each learning cycle.
readBlogPage.article3Body3: When learning is scheduled as a regular activity, pressure drops and accumulated results become clearer.
readBlogPage.article4Body1: Small apartments need clear zoning. When each item has a specific role, the space feels easier to breathe without removing all personality.
readBlogPage.article4Body2: Natural light, closed storage and a restrained color palette reduce clutter. Small accents like artwork, plants or textiles can keep the space warm.
readBlogPage.article4Body3: Effective minimalism is not emptiness; it is keeping what is actually used and meaningful.
readBlogPage.article5Body1: Personalized news feeds are moving from simple metrics to models that understand more context. Systems do not only check which articles get many views, but also how users interact over time.
readBlogPage.article5Body2: This makes content more relevant, but it also requires interfaces to be transparent about recommendations and allow users to adjust preferences.
readBlogPage.article5Body3: A good reading feed balances familiarity and discovery, avoiding pushing users into too narrow a topic range.
readBlogPage.article6Body1: A weekend trip near the coast should keep the itinerary light. Choosing fewer stops, prioritizing rest time and preparing food options in advance makes the trip more comfortable.
readBlogPage.article6Body2: If traveling by car, check the weather, route and parking before leaving. These small details help avoid wasting time after arrival.
readBlogPage.article6Body3: The most important thing is keeping the pace slow enough for the trip to actually feel like a break.
readBlogPage.article7Body1: Community football teams often struggle to keep a schedule because members have different availability. Clear role assignment reduces dependence on a single organizer.
readBlogPage.article7Body2: A consistent practice schedule needs a fixed field, reminder group and simple fitness tracking. Teams do not need complex systems to improve discipline.
readBlogPage.article7Body3: When everyone knows when they need to show up and why, practice quality becomes more stable.
readBlogPage.article8Body1: Small game development teams often start with very focused prototypes. They test ideas, controls and the core loop before expanding content.
readBlogPage.article8Body2: The strength of indie games is the ability to tell familiar stories, test unusual mechanics and respond quickly to early players.
readBlogPage.article8Body3: When scope is kept clear, a small team can still create a product with its own identity.
